subtopics.name: Circular Kinematics
learning_outcomes: define the radian and express angular displacement in radians
understand and use the concept of angular speed
recall and use ω = 2π/T and v = rω

subtopics.name: Centripetal Acceleration
learning_outcomes: understand that a force of constant magnitude that is always perpendicular to the direction of motion causes centripetal acceleration
understand that centripetal acceleration causes circular motion with a constant angular speed
recall and use a = rω² and a = v²/r
recall and use F = mrω² and F = mv²/r

subtopics.name: Gravitational Field Concepts
learning_outcomes: understand that a gravitational field is an example of a field of force and define gravitational field as force per unit mass
represent a gravitational field by means of field lines

subtopics.name: Newtonian Gravitation
learning_outcomes: understand that, for a point outside a uniform sphere, the mass of the sphere may be considered to be a point mass at its centre
recall and use Newton's law of gravitation F = Gm1m2/r² for the force between two point masses
analyse circular orbits in gravitational fields by relating the gravitational force to the centripetal acceleration it causes
understand that a satellite in a geostationary orbit remains at the same point above the Earth's surface, with an orbital period of 24 hours, orbiting from west to east, directly above the Equator

subtopics.name: Point Mass Field Strength
learning_outcomes: derive, from Newton's law of gravitation and the definition of gravitational field, the equation g = GM/r² for the gravitational field strength due to a point mass
recall and use g = GM/r²
understand why g is approximately constant for small changes in height near the Earth's surface

subtopics.name: Gravitational Potential Energy
learning_outcomes: define gravitational potential at a point as the work done per unit mass in bringing a small test mass from infinity to the point
use φ = -GM/r for the gravitational potential in the field due to a point mass
understand how the concept of gravitational potential leads to the gravitational potential energy of two point masses and use EP = -GMm/r

subtopics.name: Thermal Equilibrium
learning_outcomes: understand that (thermal) energy is transferred from a region of higher temperature to a region of lower temperature
understand that regions of equal temperature are in thermal equilibrium

subtopics.name: Thermodynamic Temperature Scales
learning_outcomes: understand that a physical property that varies with temperature may be used for the measurement of temperature and state examples of such properties
understand that the scale of thermodynamic temperature does not depend on the property of any particular substance
convert temperatures between kelvin and degrees Celsius and recall that T/K = θ/°C + 273.15
understand that the lowest possible temperature is zero kelvin on the thermodynamic temperature scale and that this is known as absolute zero

subtopics.name: Specific Heat & Latent Heat
learning_outcomes: define and use specific heat capacity
define and use specific latent heat and distinguish between specific latent heat of fusion and specific latent heat of vaporisation

subtopics.name: The Mole Concept
learning_outcomes: understand that amount of substance is an SI base quantity with the base unit mol
use molar quantities where one mole of any substance is the amount containing a number of particles of that substance equal to the Avogadro constant NA

subtopics.name: Ideal Gas Equation
learning_outcomes: understand that a gas obeying pV ∝ T where T is the thermodynamic temperature, is known as an ideal gas
recall and use the equation of state for an ideal gas expressed as pV = nRT and as pV = NkT
recall that the Boltzmann constant k is given by k = R/NA

subtopics.name: Gas Kinetic Theory
learning_outcomes: state the basic assumptions of the kinetic theory of gases
explain how molecular movement causes the pressure exerted by a gas and derive and use the relationship pV = 1/3 Nm<c²>
understand that the root-mean-square speed Cr.m.s. is given by √<c²>
compare pV = 1/3 Nm<c²> with pV = NkT to deduce that the average translational kinetic energy of a molecule is 3/2 kT

subtopics.name: Internal Energy State
learning_outcomes: understand that internal energy is determined by the state of the system and is the sum of random kinetic and potential energies associated with molecules
relate a rise in temperature of an object to an increase in its internal energy

subtopics.name: First Law of Thermodynamics
learning_outcomes: recall and use W = pΔV for work done at constant pressure
recall and use the first law of thermodynamics ΔU = q + W

subtopics.name: Simple Harmonic Motion
learning_outcomes: understand and use the terms displacement, amplitude, period, frequency, angular frequency and phase difference
understand that SHM occurs when acceleration is proportional to displacement and in the opposite direction
use a = -ω²x and x = x0 sin ωt
use the equations v = v0 cos ωt and v = ±ω√(x0² - x²)
analyse and interpret graphical representations of SHM

subtopics.name: Energy in SHM
learning_outcomes: describe the interchange between kinetic and potential energy during simple harmonic motion
recall and use for the total energy E = 1/2 mω²x0²

subtopics.name: Damping & Resonance
learning_outcomes: understand that a resistive force acting on an oscillating system causes damping
understand and use the terms light, critical and heavy damping and sketch displacement-time graphs
understand that resonance involves a maximum amplitude occurring when forced to oscillate at the natural frequency

subtopics.name: Electric Field Concepts
learning_outcomes: understand an electric field as force per unit positive charge
recall and use F = qE
represent an electric field by means of field lines

subtopics.name: Uniform Electric Fields
learning_outcomes: recall and use E = ΔV/Δd for parallel plates
describe the effect of a uniform electric field on the motion of charged particles

subtopics.name: Coulomb's Law
learning_outcomes: understand that charge on a sphere may be considered a point charge at its centre
recall and use Coulomb’s law F = Q1Q2/(4πε0r²)

subtopics.name: Point Charge Strength
learning_outcomes: recall and use E = Q/(4πε0r²) for field strength

subtopics.name: Electric Potential Energy
learning_outcomes: define electric potential as work done per unit positive charge from infinity
understand field strength as the negative of potential gradient
use V = Q/(4πε0r) and EP = Qq/(4πε0r)

subtopics.name: Capacitors in Circuits
learning_outcomes: define capacitance and use C = Q/V
derive and use formulae for combined capacitance in series and parallel

subtopics.name: Energy Stored in Capacitors
learning_outcomes: determine electric potential energy from the area under a potential-charge graph
recall and use W = 1/2 QV = 1/2 CV²

subtopics.name: Capacitor Discharge
learning_outcomes: analyse variation with time for potential difference, charge and current
recall and use the time constant τ = RC
use equations of the form x = x0e^-(t/RC)

subtopics.name: Magnetic Field Lines
learning_outcomes: understand magnetic fields are produced by moving charges or permanent magnets
represent a magnetic field by field lines

subtopics.name: Magnetic Force on Wires
learning_outcomes: recall and use F = BIL sin θ with Fleming’s left-hand rule
define magnetic flux density

subtopics.name: Force on Moving Charges
learning_outcomes: recall and use F = BQv sin θ
understand the origin of Hall voltage and the Hall probe
describe motion in a uniform magnetic field and explain velocity selection

subtopics.name: Fields due to Currents
learning_outcomes: sketch field patterns for straight wires, coils, and solenoids
explain forces between current-carrying conductors

subtopics.name: Electromagnetic Induction
learning_outcomes: define magnetic flux and flux linkage
recall and use Faraday’s and Lenz’s laws

subtopics.name: RMS & Peak Values
learning_outcomes: understand period, frequency and peak value
use sinusoidal equations x = x0 sin ωt
distinguish between r.m.s. and peak values for power, current, and voltage

subtopics.name: AC Rectification & Smoothing
learning_outcomes: distinguish between half-wave and full-wave rectification
explain the use of bridge rectifiers and capacitor smoothing

subtopics.name: Photon Energy & Momentum
learning_outcomes: understand the particulate nature of radiation and the photon as a quantum of energy
recall and use E = hf and the unit electronvolt (eV)
understand photon momentum p = E/c

subtopics.name: Photoelectric Effect
learning_outcomes: understand threshold frequency and wavelength
explain photoelectric emission using work function energy
recall and use hf = Φ + 1/2 mv²max

subtopics.name: Wave-Particle Duality
learning_outcomes: understand evidence for particulate and wave natures
interpret electron diffraction and de Broglie wavelength
recall and use λ = h/p

subtopics.name: Atomic Energy Levels
learning_outcomes: understand discrete energy levels and line spectra
recall and use hf = E1 - E2

subtopics.name: Mass Defect & Binding Energy
learning_outcomes: understand E = mc² and mass-energy equivalence
represent nuclear reactions using equations
define mass defect, binding energy, fusion and fission

subtopics.name: Radioactive Decay Kinetics
learning_outcomes: understand the random and spontaneous nature of decay
define activity, decay constant and half-life
use exponential decay equations and A = λN

subtopics.name: Ultrasound Imaging
learning_outcomes: understand piezoelectric transducers and ultrasound generation
define acoustic impedance and intensity reflection coefficients
use the attenuation equation I = I0e^-μx

subtopics.name: X-ray & CT Scanning
learning_outcomes: explain X-ray production and calculate minimum wavelength
understand contrast and attenuation in X-ray imaging
describe the principles of Computed Tomography (CT)

subtopics.name: PET Scanning & Annihilation
learning_outcomes: understand tracers and β+ decay
explain electron-positron annihilation and gamma-ray production
calculate photon energy and understand image creation from arrival times

subtopics.name: Luminosity & Standard Candles
learning_outcomes: define luminosity and radiant flux intensity
understand the use of standard candles to determine galactic distances

subtopics.name: Stellar Radii Laws
learning_outcomes: recall and use Wien’s displacement law
use the Stefan-Boltzmann law L = 4πσr²T⁴ to estimate radius

subtopics.name: Hubble’s Law & Redshift
learning_outcomes: understand redshift in spectra and use the redshift equation
recall and use Hubble’s law v ≈ H0d and explain the Big Bang theory
